newsSince: No new single contract since June 9, but SCREEN's FY3/27 guidance (sales +19.7% to JPY725B, OP +22.4%) reaffirms strong AI-driven HBM/DDR/foundry and OLED capex, supporting the memory-capex linkage. Shares are caught in the same US legislative export-control push aimed at WFE sales to China (House Select Committee / MATCH Act).

sources.label: House China hawks urge bans on chipmaking equipment (The Hill)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens: FY3/27 guidance reaffirming HBM/DDR/foundry capex keeps SCREEN a direct memory-capex beneficiary, a modest fundamental positive vs Jun 9. But the new US export-control legislative push (MATCH Act) is an emerging China-WFE risk; +102% YTD already prices the upside, so hold.
verdicts.bull: FY3/27 guide +20% sales confirms AI-fab/HBM capex ramp; ~45% share in cleaning tools makes SCREEN a direct AI-node beneficiary.
verdicts.neutral: Guidance reaffirms a known thesis; melt-up since Jun 9 is largely sector flow rather than new orders.
verdicts.bear: US legislative push to extend China WFE bans threatens order book; expectations now elevated at +102% YTD.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: HBM/DDR capacity adds; next quarterly orders
verdicts.primaryRisk: US/Japan China export-control tightening; capex-cycle timing

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: SCREEN is an indirect cleaning/process-complexity beneficiary, not optics-specific. FY3/27 guidance is supportive of front-end intensity but offers no new co-packaged-optics angle since Jun 9. Hold; the melt-up makes it more crowded, not more CPO-relevant.
verdicts.bull: Process-complexity at advanced nodes/packaging keeps cleaning demand structurally high.
verdicts.neutral: No CPO-specific catalyst; relevance to this lens is indirect and unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Pure capex-cycle name with no optical/packaging hook; +102% YTD adds crowding.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Capex cycle; no direct CPO exposure
